title.en: AI App Deployment: Serving, APIs & Cost Optimization
title.ta: AI App Deployment: Serving, APIs & செலவு மேம்படுத்தல்

description.en: Ship your AI app to production — learn API wrappers with FastAPI, cost optimization strategies, caching, and monitoring.
description.ta: உங்கள் AI app-ஐ production-க்கு அனுப்புங்கள் — FastAPI உடன் API wrappers, செலவு optimization உத்திகள், caching, monitoring கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Launch Pad

Vaathiyaar stands before a rocket control panel. "Students, building an AI demo is easy. Deploying it for REAL USERS who pay REAL MONEY — that's the challenge. Today you'll learn to wrap your AI in a FastAPI server, optimize costs, add caching, and monitor everything."

### The Production Checklist

1. **API Wrapper** — Wrap your AI logic in a FastAPI endpoint
2. **Cost Control** — Cache responses, use cheaper models when possible
3. **Rate Limiting** — Prevent abuse and control costs
4. **Error Handling** — Graceful fallbacks when the AI API is down
5. **Monitoring** — Track latency, token usage, error rates
6. **Security** — API keys, authentication, input validation

### Cost Optimization Strategies

| Strategy | Savings | Effort |
|----------|---------|--------|
| Cache common queries | 40-60% | Low |
| Use gpt-4o-mini for simple tasks | 90% vs gpt-4o | Low |
| Reduce max_tokens | 10-30% | Low |
| Batch requests | 20-40% | Medium |
| Self-host open-source models | 70-90% | High |

> **Key Insight:** The #1 cost optimization is caching. If 100 users ask the same question, you should call the LLM once and cache the result.
story_variants.ta:
## வாத்தியாரின் Launch Pad

வாத்தியார் ஒரு rocket control panel முன் நிற்கிறார். "மாணவர்களே, AI demo உருவாக்குவது எளிது. உண்மையான பயனர்களுக்கு deploy செய்வது — அதுதான் சவால்."

explanation.en: FastAPI for building the API server — fast, typed, auto-documented
OpenAI client for the AI backend
lru_cache for simple in-memory response caching
Initialize OpenAI client once — reused across requests
Typed request model — FastAPI validates automatically
Default to the cheapest model — override for complex tasks
Limit response length to control costs
Now the cached AI call function
Track token usage for cost monitoring
Whether this response came from cache

animation_sequence.left_content:
Model: gpt-4o ($2.50/1M input)
No caching: 10,000 calls/day
max_tokens: 2000 (default)
Monthly cost: ~$750

# Every request hits the API
# No monitoring, no fallbacks
# One model for all tasks
animation_sequence.right_content:
Model: gpt-4o-mini ($0.15/1M input)
Caching: 60% hit rate → 4,000 calls/day
max_tokens: 500 (optimized)
Monthly cost: ~$18

# 97.6% cost reduction!
# Cache common queries
# Monitor & alert on anomalies

output: >>> # Second identical request — served from cache!

instruction.en: Build a complete FastAPI AI app with: 1) A /chat endpoint that takes a message and returns an AI response, 2) Simple dictionary-based caching, 3) Request logging with timestamp and token count, 4) A /stats endpoint that shows total requests, cache hit rate, and total tokens used.
instruction.ta: முழுமையான FastAPI AI app உருவாக்குங்கள்: 1) message எடுத்து AI response திருப்பி அனுப்பும் /chat endpoint, 2) Dictionary-based caching, 3) timestamp மற்றும் token count உடன் request logging, 4) மொத்த requests, cache hit rate, tokens காட்டும் /stats endpoint.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from openai import OpenAI
import time

app = FastAPI()
client = OpenAI()

# Simple cache
cache = {}
stats = {'total_requests': 0, 'cache_hits': 0, 'total_tokens': 0}

class ChatRequest(BaseModel):
    message: str

class ChatResponse(BaseModel):
    reply: str
    cached: bool
    latency_ms: float

@app.post('/chat', response_model=ChatResponse)
async def chat(request: ChatRequest):
    stats['total_requests'] += 1
    start = time.time()
    
    # Check cache first
    # If not cached, call OpenAI
    # Update stats
    # Return response
    pass

@app.get('/stats')
async def get_stats():
    # Return stats with cache hit rate
    pass

# Run: uvicorn main:app --reload
practice_challenges.expected_output:
POST /chat -> {reply, cached, latency_ms}
GET /stats -> {total_requests, cache_hits, hit_rate, total_tokens}

en: Check cache with: if request.message in cache
Use time.time() before and after the API call for latency
ta: Cache சரிபார்: if request.message in cache
Latency-க்கு API call-க்கு முன்/பின் time.time() பயன்படுத்துங்கள்

q.en: What is the #1 cost optimization for AI APIs?
Why use FastAPI for AI app deployment?
q.ta: AI app deployment-க்கு FastAPI ஏன் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்?

en: It's fast, has automatic validation, and generates API docs
ta: வேகமானது, automatic validation உள்ளது, API docs உருவாக்குகிறது

q.en: How much can you save by switching from gpt-4o to gpt-4o-mini for simple tasks?
q.ta: எளிய பணிகளுக்கு gpt-4o-விலிருந்து gpt-4o-mini-க்கு மாறுவதன் மூலம் எவ்வளவு சேமிக்கலாம்?
